<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_customer_purchase_history {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Customer, "customer_id"> customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  double total_amount;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Order, "order_id"> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Product, "product_id"> product_id;
  int quantity;
};

struct PurchaseHistory {
  std::string product_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  int quantity;
};

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create tables by writing empty vectors
  sqlgen::write(conn, std::vector<Customer>{});
  sqlgen::write(conn, std::vector<Order>{});
  sqlgen::write(conn, std::vector<OrderItem>{});
  sqlgen::write(conn, std::vector<Product>{});

  // Build the query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product, "t4">(
      "name"_t4.as<"product_name">(),
      "order_date"_t2,
      "quantity"_t3)
      | sqlgen::inner_join<OrderItem, "t3">("product_id"_t4 == "product_id"_t3)
      | sqlgen::inner_join<Order, "t2">("order_id"_t3 == "order_id"_t2)
      | sqlgen::inner_join<Customer, "t1">("customer_id"_t2 == "customer_id"_t1)
      | sqlgen::where("customer_id"_t1 == customer_id)
      | sqlgen::order_by("order_date"_t2.desc());

  // Execute the query and return the result
  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace delete_with_complex_condition {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int age;
  std::string status;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates deleting with complex conditions.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string delete_with_complex_condition() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    
    auto condition = ("age"_c < 18) || ("status"_c == "inactive");
    auto delete_stmt = sqlgen::delete_from<User> | sqlgen::where(condition);
    
    return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(delete_stmt);
}

}  // namespace delete_with_complex_condition
```</answer>

     auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace drop_table_if_exists {

struct Log {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string message;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates dropping a table if it exists.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string drop_table_if_exists() {
    auto drop_obj = sqlgen::drop<Log> | sqlgen::if_exists;
    return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(drop_obj);
}

}  // namespace drop_table_if_exists
```</answer>
